name: cf-auto-review

criteria.name: four_layer_coverage
criteria.description: Review covers all 4 layers: correctness, security, performance, maintainability

scoring.0: Only covers one layer (e.g., only style/formatting comments)
scoring.1: Covers two layers but completely ignores the others
scoring.2: Covers three layers but misses one with relevant issues
scoring.3: All four layers addressed: correctness, security, performance, and maintainability

criteria.name: issue_quality
criteria.description: Reported issues are real, significant, and well-explained

scoring.0: Issues are nitpicks or style preferences with no real impact
scoring.1: Mix of real issues and noise — hard to tell which matter
scoring.2: Issues are real and significant but explanations could be clearer
scoring.3: Every issue is significant, clearly explained, and includes the potential impact

criteria.name: no_false_positives
criteria.description: Does not flag correct, idiomatic code as problematic

scoring.0: Majority of findings are false positives or misunderstandings of the code
scoring.1: Several false positives that undermine trust in the review
scoring.2: One or two borderline false positives but otherwise accurate
scoring.3: Zero false positives — every finding is a genuine concern

criteria.name: severity_accuracy
criteria.description: Issue severity levels (critical/warning/info) accurately reflect actual impact

scoring.0: Severity levels are random or inverted (critical for style, info for security bugs)
scoring.1: Most issues are labeled the same severity regardless of actual impact
scoring.2: Severity is mostly accurate but one issue is over- or under-rated
scoring.3: Severity levels precisely match the actual impact of each issue

notes: Auto-review is triggered automatically, not by the user. It should be efficient and not over-report. Clean code with no issues should produce a short 'no issues found' report rather than fabricating problems.
